page: Microsoft Azure L4aos_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 4 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and a base clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It also includes 2880 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not feature any GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and bzip3 single-threaded decompression scoring in the top 25%. However, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth present notable tradeoffs. Multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core, and large-block memory write bandwidth fall into the average tier, while read-only memory bandwidth and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression are weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Software compilation with Clang and database operations also show weaker performance profiles, and LLM inference on medium 7B models is poor to weak.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the L4aos_v4 offers a high density of local storage and memory relative to its 4 vCPUs, making it a resource-dense option for workloads requiring large datasets but moderate compute scaling. Given the strong single-core execution and high storage capacity, this server is well-suited for memory-intensive single-threaded applications, text processing, and caching layers like Redis. Conversely, it is less optimal for highly parallelized multi-threaded tasks, heavy database transaction processing, or large-scale LLM inference due to its memory bandwidth and multi-core performance limitations.
description: The Microsoft Azure L4aos_v4 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 4 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and a 3.7 GHz clock speed. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM and a substantial 2880 GB of local storage, with Premium Storage capability. While single-core benchmarks, such as Geekbench single-core and text processing, rank in the top 25%, the instance exhibits average multi-core performance and weak read-only memory bandwidth. This hardware profile delivers high resource density for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads. It is highly suitable for text processing, Redis caching, and memory-optimized single-threaded applications, though less effective for heavy database operations or multi-threaded compilation tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L4aos_v4 is a memory-optimized VM featuring a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 CPU, 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 2880 GB of local storage. It offers strong single-core performance with average multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L4aos_v4 is an x86_64 VM with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 2880 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine combining strong single-core AMD EPYC performance with high-capacity local storage for resource-dense workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
Generous 2880 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Tradeoffs include weak read-only memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling
Best suited for caching, text processing, and memory-intensive single-threaded tasks
